_note: Agent frameworks (programmatic, not chat hosts). 2026-Q2 snapshot.

mcpSupport.note: MCP servers can be wrapped as LangChain tools; integration improving but not yet first-class.

complexity.note: Many abstractions; unwinding them when something breaks takes effort.

lagsAt: Documentation churn (APIs shift between minor versions)
Sometimes you fight the abstraction more than the problem
axes.verdict: Default if you need many integrations out of the box and have the team capacity to manage abstraction depth.

mcpSupport.note: MCP server integration is straightforward; LlamaIndex tools wrap MCP servers cleanly.

lagsAt: Less general-purpose than LangChain for non-RAG agents
axes.verdict: Default when retrieval is the dominant pattern. Less compelling for pure tool-call agents.

mcpSupport.note: No native MCP integration; you wrap tools yourself.

complexity.note: Different mental model — you compose modules, optimisers tune prompts for you.

leadsAt: Cleaner code for production agents than ad-hoc prompts
axes.verdict: Default when prompt quality matters more than framework convenience. Production-friendly once internalised.

mcpSupport.note: Pydantic AI has been rapid on MCP integration; check current docs for latest.

complexity.note: Smaller surface, type-driven. Easier to pick up than LangChain.

leadsAt: Familiarity if you already use Pydantic / FastAPI
lagsAt: Newer; smaller integration catalog than LangChain
axes.verdict: Default for Python teams who already build with Pydantic and want minimal magic.

leadsAt: Successor to AutoGen with cleaner abstractions
lagsAt: Newer; ecosystem still settling post-AutoGen consolidation
axes.verdict: Default if you're in the Microsoft / Azure stack and need .NET. Worth comparing against LangChain if you're Python-only.
